output.changeValidated: font-bold removed from TextCard titles. Typography tokens (text-header-1, text-header-3) define font-weight: 400. Titles should render at weight 400 instead of 700.
output.overallAssessment: The TextCard titles render visually identically before and after the font-bold removal. The heading font is Anton, which is a single-weight display font -- it only ships one weight, so font-weight: 400 and font-weight: 700 produce the exact same visual output. The implementation matches the Figma design across all breakpoints and browsers.

typographyVerification.fontCharacteristic: Anton is a single-weight display font. It only has one weight variant, so font-weight values (400 vs 700) produce identical visual rendering.
typographyVerification.visualResult: Titles appear heavy/bold in both Figma and implementation because Anton is inherently a heavy-weight typeface. No visual difference from removing font-bold.

positiveFindings.description: TextCard titles render with correct heavy visual weight across all browsers. Anton font's single-weight nature means the font-bold removal has zero visual impact, which is the correct behavior -- the typography tokens already define the appropriate weight.
Cross-browser consistency is excellent. Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit all render the TextCard titles identically at every breakpoint.
Overall composition matches the Figma design. Card layouts, masonry patterns, image placement, and text positioning are all consistent with the reference designs.
